name: Christ's Home Community Center
description: Christ's Home Community Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Warminster, PA. There are 3 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play. Players have access to food, restrooms, water, and trainers or lessons.

name: Diane
description: Come play pickleball at Diane in Newtown, PA! There are 2 outdoor hard courts. You’ll have to create your own lines and bring your own net. You can’t reserve the courts. The courts are private.

name: Doylestown Pickleball
description: Please visit www.DoylestownPickleball.com to reserve your court or signup for play.

name: Doylestown Tennis Club
description: Doylestown Tennis Club is a place to play pickleball in Doylestown, PA. There are 8 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Amenities include a pro shop or equipment store, restrooms, and water. A membership is required to play.

name: Doylestown YMCA
description: Come play pickleball at Doylestown YMCA in Doylestown, PA! There are 9 hard courts. 3 are indoors and 6 are outdoors. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts can be reserved. A membership is required to play. Amenities include locker rooms, restrooms, water, and trainers or lessons.
fees: Members only — Members play free. Non-member may access courts with guest fee

name: Fairless Hills YMCA
description:
YMCA of Bucks and Hunterdon Counties offers a variety of pickleball group play, instructional classes and special events. 

Schedules are available online here: ymcabhc.org/schedules

name: Firefighters' Park
description: Firefighters' Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Langhorne, PA. There are 8 outdoor hard and concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. Amenities include lights.

name: Guinea Lane Park
description: Courts no longer seem to be there. fences have gone up and the nets and the paint are gone
hours: Monday - Thursday from 8am - 4pm Friday from 7am - 3pm Contact Us Warwick Township ParkRec 1733 Township Greene Jamison, PA 18929 Email: dsharapan@warwickbucks.gov Phone: 215-343-6100 Fax: 215-491-2972 Connect with Us © 2026 Warwick Township ParkRec Terms ○ Privacy ○ Help ○ How To Provided by MyRec.com Recreation Software

name: Kulp Park
description: Kulp Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Perkasie, PA. There are 12 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Players have access to restrooms, lights, and wheelchair accessibility. The courts are free. Reservations are not available.

name: Lower Makefield Township Pickleball Club
description: Lower Makefield Township Pickleball Club is a place to play pickleball in Yardley, PA. There are 4 outdoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Reservations are available. A membership is required to play.

name: Middletown  senior center
description:
3 courts that are open to the public.
Or you can go on Middletown township website for more information.
open_play_schedule: Can play anytime they have lights but they are very good. But courts are nice level permanent  lines n nets.

name: Pennsbury Racquet & Athletic Club
description: Pennsbury Racquet & Athletic Club is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Morrisville, PA. There are 12 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, lights, trainers or lessons, a pro shop or equipment store, and locker rooms. You can reserve the courts.

name: Quaker Penn Park
description: Quaker Penn Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Morrisville, PA. There are 4 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free.

name: Quakertown YMCA
description:
YMCA of Bucks and Hunterdon Counties offers pickleball courts at our Bucks and Hunterdon County courts located in Doylestown, Fairless Hills and Quakertown, Pennsylvania as well as Flemington, New Jersey.  Members access courts for free and non-members may pay a guest rate for court access. Information about open play as well as clinics, group classes, private/semi-private lessons and tournaments can be found online here: ymcabhc.org/pickleball. 

Questions? Email us at pickleball@ymcabhc.org
fees: Members only — Members play free. Non-member may access courts with guest fee

name: Revere Road Courts
description: Revere Road Courts offers 3 tennis courts with permanent pickleball lines.

name: Robert O Keller Memorial Park
description: Come play pickleball at Robert O Keller Memorial Park in Quakertown, PA! There are 8 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. Players have access to restrooms, water, and lights.

name: Southampton Community Center Park
description: Southampton Community Center Park is a place to play pickleball in Southampton, PA. There are 2 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play. Players have access to restrooms, water, and lights.

name: The Garden of Reflection 9-11 Memorial Park
description: There are technically 4 courts but one has no net.

name: The Tennis Zone & Fitness Center
description: The Tennis Zone & Fitness Center is a place to play pickleball in Quakertown, PA. There are 2 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Amenities include restrooms. A membership is required to play.

name: Unami Creek Park
description: Come play pickleball at Unami Creek Park in Quakertown, PA! There are 2 outdoor courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free.
